Client Onboarding Guide

Gmail Forwarding
Setup

A step-by-step walkthrough for routing your calendar booking emails from Gmail straight into Contente — automatically, every time.

Prepared for {{ clientName }}
~10 minutes
5 simple phases
One short pause for approval
The path ahead

Five phases, start to finish

1
Add the routing address
Register your Contente address in Gmail.
2
Required pause
Request approval
Email us, then pause up to 24 hours.
3
Define the trigger
Tell Gmail which emails to act on.
4
Route the emails
Forward them to Contente & save.
5
Confirm & test
Tell us it's live — we run a test.
1
Phase One

Add the routing address

Inside Gmail's settings, you'll register the unique Contente address that your bookings will be forwarded to.

In Gmail, click the gear icon → See all settings. Then open the Forwarding and POP/IMAP tab along the top. This is all done on a computer — the full settings menu isn't available in the phone app.
Gmail Forwarding Setup · Phase 1
Click Add a forwarding address, paste your exact Contente address, then click Next.
This address is unique to you. Please confirm it matches exactly what we sent you, then paste it in. Yours is unique — we send it to you directly alongside this guide. Paste it in exactly; the one shown is only an example.
Gmail Forwarding Setup · Phase 1
Google may pop up a "Verify it's you" screen. Just follow the on-screen prompt — usually confirming on your phone. This is a standard Google security step. Once cleared, the address is added.
Gmail Forwarding Setup · Phase 1
2
Phase Two · Required pause

Request approval

The address you just added must be approved on our end before the next phase will work.

! Stop here — required pause

Don't create the filter yet

The address you added needs to be approved on our side first. Email our team, then pause while we verify it.

1 Email us

Send a quick note that you've added the address.

2 Wait for the go-ahead

We approve the address on our backend, then give you the go-ahead.

Up to 24 hours before you continue

Only once we confirm should you move on to Phase 3.

3
Phase Three

Define the trigger

Now that the address is approved, create a filter so Gmail knows exactly which booking emails to forward.

In Settings, open the Filters and Blocked Addresses tab. Then click Create a new filter.
1 · Open the tab
2 · Create a new filter
Gmail Forwarding Setup · Phase 3
From — and only this sender

The exact address your booking notifications arrive from — e.g. appointments@schedulingapp.com

Has the words — a booking keyword

A word unique to booking emails — e.g. appointment or scheduled

Send yourself a test booking so a real notification lands — then copy the sender address and keyword straight from it. Then click Create filter.
Gmail Forwarding Setup · Phase 3
4
Phase Four

Route the emails

The final connection — tell Gmail to forward every matching email to your approved Contente address.

Tick Forward it to: and choose your approved Contente address from the dropdown. Then click Create filter. The address only appears in this list because we approved it in Phase 2.
Gmail Forwarding Setup · Phase 4
5
Phase Five

Confirm & test

One last email so we know it's live — then we run a test booking to confirm it works.

Final step

We confirm it's live

Email us once your filter is set up and we'll run a test booking to confirm appointments are reaching Contente — and showing up in your dashboard.

Let us know
Your dashboard
